tags.name: Description
tags.value: In response to AR.IO (ArNS) migrating its registry to Solana, AO launched its own name system, apparently aggregating names from various protocols (ANS, Argora etc.).  The list seems to be missing my ANS-names - I'm pretty sure I had a few that didn't collide with my ArNS-ones.

tags.name: File-Name
tags.value: 2026-06-02 - x.com - ao X_ssä_ _Introducing Permaweb Names_ _ X [Website Screenshot 17964].png

tags.name: Description
tags.value: AO making a further post regarding their replacement for ArNS on X/Twitter. It seems that the system is now live with a resolver/management page found at https://ao.arweave.net/#/names (though it appears to be sluggish and only working randomly, data fetches failing as is usual for AO-stuff). It is quite obvious that the other team mentioned in the article is AR.IO who originally created ArNS and is now migrating its registry to Solana - this post was published soon after they took a snapshot of their registry. All my ArNS-names appear to included in the new system (my ANS-names don't seem to be there yet - I recall I had some unique ones even while many were same as on the ArNS-side). Getting all the names as permanent is a nice thing, (hopefully) permanently securing sart and finalserenity at least on the AO-side. It also seems that with this, sart.arweave.net now properly resolves to 0.7.3 - it was previously lagging a few version behind. A positive thing overall, though I feel a bit bad for AR.IO - I don't doubt that they did all that they could to try to get things to work on HyperBEAM.
